# Questions in Livestream 18

Live Stream #18: For the Good Life

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=296" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:56</a> | If some Israelites were literate in Egyptian, could the original Torah have been written in Egyptian before being copied into Paleo-Hebrew? | He said that was unlikely. The Torah does contain Egyptian loanwords, but its syntax does not look like a work first composed in Egyptian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=458" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:38</a> | Besides Papyrus Amherst 63, are there other texts that combine Jewish or Hebrew patterns with Egyptian script? | He said Papyrus Amherst 63 is very unusual and he did not know of many real parallels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=740" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:20</a> | What does he think of the reported space-rock destruction at Tell el-Hammam, and does it fit Sodom and Gomorrah at all? | He was cautious. He had concerns about the article's methodology and wanted more response from specialists before accepting the skyburst claim or the Sodom identification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=1091" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:11</a> | Does he think more royal tombs will be found in the Valley of the Kings, and which New Kingdom kings' tombs or mummies are still missing? | Probably yes in a broad sense. He doubted many more kingly tombs remain, but thought more royal burials may still be there; he named Amenhotep I, either Akhenaten or Smenkhkare, and Ramesses VIII as major missing cases. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=1433" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:53</a> | How likely is it that the Tanakh was ever translated into Egyptian in antiquity, given Jewish temples in places like Elephantine and Leontopolis? | He thought that was unlikely in earlier Egyptian, though he noted that Old Testament material was later translated into Coptic, the last phase of Egyptian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=1610" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:50</a> | If the Avaris Israelites were literate in Egyptian, would the Ten Commandments written by the finger of the Lord originally have been in Egyptian? | He said no. Avaris was multilingual, but after leaving Egypt the Israelites would mostly have used a West Semitic dialect, so he expected the commandments in early Hebrew rather than Egyptian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=1721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:41</a> | What was the extent of Egyptian cultural and linguistic influence on Judah during the 26th dynasty, especially under Necho II and Apries? | He said Necho II is the clearer case because the historical evidence is better, but the subject remains patchy and not especially well covered. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=1987" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:07</a> | Did Jesus's family's time in Egypt leave any clear Egyptian influence on Jesus? | He said no clear Egyptian influence can be identified. Jewish communities in Roman Egypt were culturally distinct enough that this would not be expected. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=2185" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:25</a> | Speaking of tombs, what is his favorite tomb in the Valley of the Kings? | He favored one of the later Ramesside tombs and especially praised the beauty of the dynasty 20 royal tombs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=2369" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:29</a> | Should elim and bene elohim in Psalm 29:1 and Job 1:6 be translated as sons of God or sons of gods? | He said Psalm 29:1 is best understood as "sons of gods," while Job 1:6 uses a different construction and should be read as "sons of God." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=2700" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:00</a> | What is the best book on KV5, and what about the popular-level book on it? | He said he had not seen the preliminary report, but pointed to major Theban Mapping Project work as the more dependable direction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=2859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:39</a> | Is KV5 the site Kent Weeks has been working on for nearly thirty years? | Yes. Falk said Weeks excavated KV5, recognized it was more than a small foyer, and also led major Theban Mapping Project work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=2920" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:40</a> | What was the late relationship between Egypt and Judah under Zedekiah and Hophra, politically, economically, and socially? | He said the relationship centered on Judah's attempted alliance with Egypt against Babylon, but the details are difficult to pin down. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=3164" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:44</a> | Are there Babylonian exilic loanwords in the Torah? | He said no in any strong sense. Similarities usually reflect shared Semitic roots rather than clear borrowing from the exile. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=3355" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:55</a> | What does he think of attempts to connect Tell el-Hammam's destruction with both Sodom and Jericho's collapsed wall? | He rejected that connection. Jericho's relevant destruction horizon is far too early to match the proposed Tell el-Hammam event. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=3512" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:32</a> | Do the Elephantine Jews prove pre-exilic polytheism, including belief that Yahweh had a wife? | He said Elephantine does show Jewish polytheistic practice, but that is not surprising because the Bible itself already describes widespread pre-exilic polytheism in Israel and Judah. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=3872" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:32</a> | Is the Abraham-Hammurabi synchronism contested? | Yes, heavily. He said both skeptical scholars and conservative scholars contest it, though for different reasons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=4079" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:59</a> | Does the Middle Kingdom get short shrift? | He said not really. It seems less glamorous and has fewer surviving monuments, but it remains important and well worth study. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=4297" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:37</a> | Are there Old Persian loanwords in the Torah? | He said no. He said Persian loanwords appear in later works such as Ezra, Nehemiah, and perhaps Daniel, but not in the Torah. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=4357" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:37</a> | Why did Moses have to remove his sandals at the burning bush? | Because he was entering holy ground. Falk connected it with sacred-space rules, including priestly barefoot practice in both Israel and Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=4533" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:33</a> | Does he have an opinion on the book of Daniel, or recommended reading on it? | Yes. He recommended a conservative volume by Wiseman, Fairman, and Kitchen as a strong treatment of Daniel's dating and historical issues. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=4616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:56</a> | Why did Babylonians fear lunar eclipses? | Because eclipses were abnormal, unpredictable disruptions of the expected heavenly cycles and so were taken as bad omens. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=4820" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:20</a> | Are there any documentaries on Egypt or other ancient kingdoms on Netflix or other streaming services that he would recommend for teenagers? | He could not recommend Netflix titles because he does not watch it, but he did mention a few Egypt documentaries he thought were good. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=4971" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:51</a> | Does "the stone the builders rejected has become the head cornerstone" only make sense with a pyramid, and were pyramids known throughout the ancient Near East? | He said no. Pyramids were rare outside Egypt and Nubia, ziggurats are not the same thing, and cornerstones can belong to temples and other building projects too. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=5203" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:43</a> | After Israel first reached the promised land and then wandered forty years, does the Bible imply they went back into Sinai? | He said the text does seem to place them back in the Sinai region, especially around Kadesh-barnea. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=5321" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:41</a> | Why did the Jews have to wear phylacteries during the Shema? | He treated phylacteries as a concrete ritual reminder of covenant obligations, not as something connected to the lunar-eclipse discussion that prompted the follow-up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=5582" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:02</a> | Where does the idea of Samaritan gods with dog heads and human bodies in 2 Kings 17:31 come from? | He said he did not know. After the verse was supplied, he said he did not see that detail in the text and was not familiar enough with the Avites, Nibhaz, or Tartak to explain the origin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/N4pgeufCqLQ?t=5791" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:31</a> | What is written on the pyramids? | Very little is written on real pyramids. He said Pyramid Texts begin inside pyramids with Unis, later developing into Coffin Texts and the Book of the Dead. |
